noncomputable def sectionsCast (L : X.Modules) {i j : ‚Ąē} (h : i = j) :
    sectionDeg L i ‚ČÉ‚āó[‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] sectionDeg L j :=
  match j, h with
  | _, rfl => LinearEquiv.refl _ _

/-- The transport along the reflexive equality `rfl : i = i` equals the identity automorphism
(`lem:sectionsCast_refl`).  Section-level analogue of `TensorPower.cast_refl`. -/
@[simp] lemma sectionsCast_refl (L : X.Modules) (i : ‚Ąē) :
    sectionsCast L (rfl : i = i) = LinearEquiv.refl _ (sectionDeg L i) :=
  rfl

/-- The section transport `sectionsCast L h` is `őď(X,-)` (evaluation at the top open of the
underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism) applied to the canonical index isomorphism
`eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) h) : L^{‚äói} ‚ČÖ L^{‚äój}`.  This is the identification that lets the
coherence proofs (`sectionsMul_*`) cancel the `eqToIso` reindexing baked into `tensorPowAdd`
against the outer `sectionsCast`.  Proved by `subst`: at `rfl` both sides are the identity. -/
lemma sectionsCast_eq_eqToIso (L : X.Modules) {i j : ‚Ąē} (h : i = j) (x : sectionDeg L i) :
    sectionsCast L h x =
      ((eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) h)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x := by
  subst h
  rfl

/-- Cancellation of the `tensorPowAdd`-style reindexing against the outer section transport: for
`h : j = i`, transporting along `h` undoes `őď` of the index isomorphism induced by `h.symm`.  This
is the precise shape consumed by `sectionsMul_one_mul` / `sectionsMul_mul_one` (where `tensorPowAdd`
ends in `eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (¬∑).symm)`). -/
lemma sectionsCast_eqToIso_cancel (L : X.Modules) {i j : ‚Ąē} (h : j = i) (y : sectionDeg L i) :
    sectionsCast L h
        (((eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) h.symm)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom y) = y := by
  subst h
  rfl

/-- Cast-mediated equality in the graded sigma type: if `a.fst = b.fst` and the section-component
transport maps `a.snd` to `b.snd`, then `a = b` as dependent pairs (`lem:gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast`).
Section-level analogue of `gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast` from `TensorPower.Basic` (line 123 there). -/
lemma gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast (L : X.Modules) {a b : GradedMonoid (sectionDeg L)}
    (h : a.1 = b.1) (h2 : sectionsCast L h a.2 = b.2) : a = b := by
  obtain ‚ü®i, x‚ü© := a
  obtain ‚ü®j, y‚ü© := b
  obtain rfl : i = j := h
  rw [sectionsCast_refl, LinearEquiv.refl_apply] at h2
  subst h2
  rfl

/-- Degreewise graded multiplication on section components:
`sectionDeg L i √ó sectionDeg L j ‚Üí sectionDeg L (i+j)`, defined as the composition
`őď(őľ_{i,j}) ‚ąė sectionsMul` applied to `a ‚äó‚āú b`.  Required for the coherence lemma signatures. -/
noncomputable instance (L : X.Modules) : GradedMonoid.GMul (sectionDeg L) where
  mul {i j} (a : sectionDeg L i) (b : sectionDeg L j) :=
    ((tensorPowAdd L i j).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
      ((sectionsMul (tensorPow L i) (tensorPow L j)).hom
        (a ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] b))

/-- Graded unit in degree 0: the image of `1 ‚ąą őď(X,ūĚí™_X)` in `sectionDeg L 0 = őď(X, L^{‚äó0})`
via the canonical `őďūĚí™`-module isomorphism.  Required for the coherence lemma signatures. -/
noncomputable instance (L : X.Modules) : GradedMonoid.GOne (sectionDeg L) where
  one := (1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))

/-- Definitional unfolding of the graded multiplication: `a * b` is the section
multiplication of the components followed by `őď` of the tensor-power comparison
`őľ_{i,j}`.  Holds by `rfl` and is the reuse handle for the bilinearity (`GSemiring`)
fields below. -/
lemma gMul_def (L : X.Modules) {i j : ‚Ąē} (a : sectionDeg L i) (b : sectionDeg L j) :
    GradedMonoid.GMul.mul a b
      = ((tensorPowAdd L i j).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          ((sectionsMul (tensorPow L i) (tensorPow L j)).hom
            (a ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] b)) :=
  rfl

/-! ### Helpers for the coherence proofs

The coherences (`sectionsMul_*`) all reduce, after cancelling the `tensorPowAdd`
reindexing against the outer `sectionsCast`, to an identity of the shape
`őď(structural-iso)(ő∑(elementary tensor)) = ‚Ä¶`, which is proved by `ő∑`-naturality
against the corresponding presheaf-level structural isomorphism plus a
triangle/unitor identity.  These helpers package the recurring carrier bookkeeping
(`SheafOfModules.comp_val` + `PresheafOfModules.comp_app` + the value-`ModuleCat`
diamond, handled in term mode) so the mathematical content stays visible. -/

/-- Application of a composite sheaf-of-modules morphism at the top open splits as the
composition of the applications: `őď(f ‚Čę g)(x) = őď(g)(őď(f)(x))`.  This is the non-`rfl`
split needed because the positional `comp_apply`/`hom_comp` rewrites hit the value-`ModuleCat`
diamond; the final `rfl` is the term-mode reduction. -/
lemma val_app_top_comp {A B C : X.Modules} (f : A ‚ü∂ B) (g : B ‚ü∂ C)
    (x : ‚Ü•(A.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((f ‚Čę g).val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x
      = (g.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom ((f.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x) :=
  rfl

/-- The `m = 0` branch of `tensorPowAdd`: `őľ_{0,n}` is the left unitor reindexed along
`0 + n = n`.  Holds by `rfl` (iota on the literal `0`), so it never forces the
whnf-timeout-prone unfolding of the recursive branch. -/
lemma tensorPowAdd_zero (L : X.Modules) (n : ‚Ąē) :
    tensorPowAdd L 0 n =
      tensorObjUnitIso (tensorPow L n) ‚Č™‚Čę
        eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (Nat.zero_add n).symm) :=
  rfl

/-- The hom of the left-unitor iso splits as the sheafified presheaf left unitor followed by
the sheafification counit. -/
lemma tensorObjUnitIso_hom (G : X.Modules) :
    (tensorObjUnitIso G).hom
      = sheafification.map (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom ‚Čę (sheafificationCounitIso G).hom :=
  rfl

/-- **Unitor-ő∑ core** of left unitality.  Applying `őď` of the left-unitor iso
`tensorObjUnitIso G` to the section multiplication `ő∑(1 ‚äó a)` returns `a`.

Mathematically: split `(tensorObjUnitIso G).hom = őď(őĽ_p)^# ‚Čę őĶ_G` (sheafified presheaf
left unitor, then the sheafification counit).  Unit-naturality of `ő∑` against the
presheaf left unitor `őĽ_p` (whose value at `‚ä§` sends `1 ‚äó a ‚Ü¶ 1 ‚ÄĘ a = a`) rewrites
`őď(őĽ_p)^#(ő∑(1‚äóa)) = ő∑_G(a)`; the right-triangle identity of the sheafification
adjunction then gives `őĶ_G(ő∑_G(a)) = a`. -/
lemma unitor_sectionsMul (G : X.Modules) (a : ‚Ü•(G.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((tensorObjUnitIso G).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((sectionsMul (unitModule X) G).hom
          ((1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
            ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] a)) = a := by
  -- Split the unitor into the sheafified presheaf left unitor and the counit, then peel the
  -- composite application via `val_app_top_comp` (handles the value-`ModuleCat` diamond in
  -- term mode).
  rw [tensorObjUnitIso_hom]
  -- Split the composite application `őď(f ‚Čę g)(x) = őď(g)(őď(f)(x))` in term mode (`show` reduces
  -- at default transparency, bridging the `tensorObj`/`sheafification.obj` def and the value-
  -- `ModuleCat` diamond that block positional `rw`).
  show ((sheafificationCounitIso G).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        (((sheafification.map (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom).val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          ((sectionsMul (unitModule X) G).hom
            ((1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
              ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] a))) = a
  -- Transpose identity: the composite `őĽ_p^# ‚Čę őĶ_G` is the adjunct of the presheaf left unitor
  -- `őĽ_p`.  In the adjunction `sheafification ‚ä£ forget`, `homEquiv (őĽ_p^# ‚Čę őĶ_G) = őĽ_p`: indeed
  -- `homEquiv.symm őĽ_p = (őĽ_p)^# ‚Čę őĶ_G` (`homEquiv_counit`), which is exactly the composite (the
  -- counit iso is the counit), so applying `homEquiv` to both sides gives the claim.
  have H : (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X)) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) G
        (sheafification.map (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom ‚Čę (sheafificationCounitIso G).hom)
      = (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom := by
    have hsymm : sheafification.map (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom ‚Čę (sheafificationCounitIso G).hom
        = ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
            (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
              ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X)) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) G).symm
            (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
              ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom :=
      (Adjunction.homEquiv_counit (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
        (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) _ _ _).symm
    exact ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X))
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) G).apply_eq_iff_eq_symm_apply.mpr hsymm
  -- Unfold `homEquiv` to the unit form, evaluate at the top open on `1 ‚äó a`.
  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit] at H
  have Hel := congrArg
      (fun m : (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X)) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ‚ü∂
            (toPresheafOfModules X).obj G =>
        (m.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          ((1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
            ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] a)) H
  -- The presheaf left unitor sends `1 ‚äó a ‚Ü¶ 1 ‚ÄĘ a = a`.
  have Hunit :
      ((MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
          ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] a) = a := by
    -- The presheaf left unitor at `‚ä§` is, by construction, the value-`ModuleCat` left unitor.
    have happ : (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)
          = (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
              (((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G).obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))).hom := rfl
    rw [happ]
    -- `erw` bridges the `CommRingCat`/`RingCat` tmul-key diamond; the unitor sends `1 ‚äó a ‚Ü¶ 1 ‚ÄĘ a`.
    erw [ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_hom_apply, one_smul]
  exact Hel.trans Hunit

/-- **Braiding-ő∑ core** of commutativity (`lem:sectionMul_braiding_core`).  Applying `őď` of the
braiding iso `tensorBraiding F G` to the section multiplication `ő∑(ŌÉ ‚äó ŌĄ)` swaps the factors:
it equals the section multiplication `ő∑(ŌĄ ‚äó ŌÉ)` of the swapped pair.

Mathematically this is pure unit-naturality of `ő∑` against the presheaf braiding `ő≤_p`: the
naturality square `ő≤_p ‚Čę ő∑_{G‚äó_pF} = ő∑_{F‚äó_pG} ‚Čę (ő≤_p)^#` evaluated at the top open on
`ŌÉ ‚äó ŌĄ`, using that `ő≤_p` at `‚ä§` sends `ŌÉ ‚äó ŌĄ ‚Ü¶ ŌĄ ‚äó ŌÉ`.  No counit/triangle input is needed. -/
lemma sectionMul_braiding_core (F G : X.Modules)
    (ŌÉ : ‚Ü•(F.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) (ŌĄ : ‚Ü•(G.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((tensorBraiding F G).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((sectionsMul F G).hom
          (ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ))
      = (sectionsMul G F).hom
          (ŌĄ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÉ) := by
  -- Unit naturality of `ő∑` against the presheaf braiding `ő≤_p : F‚äó_pG ‚ü∂ G‚äó_pF`.
  have H := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.naturality
    (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom
  -- Evaluate the presheaf-morphism equality at the top open on `ŌÉ ‚äó ŌĄ`.
  have Hel := congrArg
      (fun m : (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ‚ü∂
            (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ‚čô
              (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).comp
                (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj))).obj
              (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
                ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F)) =>
        (m.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          (ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ)) H
  -- The presheaf braiding at `‚ä§` sends `ŌÉ ‚äó ŌĄ ‚Ü¶ ŌĄ ‚äó ŌÉ`.
  have Hbraid :
      ((BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom.app
            (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom (ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ)
        = ŌĄ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÉ := by
    have happ : (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).hom.app
              (Opposite.op ‚ä§)
          = (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := ModuleCat (X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
              (((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))
              (((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G).obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))).hom := rfl
    rw [happ]
    rfl
  -- `Hel.symm` is, after the defeq comp-app split + the `forget/restrictScalars` identity of the
  -- right adjoint, exactly `őď(ő≤)(sectionsMul F G (ŌÉ‚äóŌĄ)) = sectionsMul G F (ő≤_p(ŌÉ‚äóŌĄ))`; rewriting
  -- the braiding image `ő≤_p(ŌÉ‚äóŌĄ) = ŌĄ‚äóŌÉ` inside `sectionsMul G F` (`Hbraid`) closes the goal.
  exact Hel.symm.trans (congrArg (fun w => (sectionsMul G F).hom w) Hbraid)

/-- **Right-unitor-ő∑ core** of right unitality (`lem:sectionMul_rightUnitor_core`).  Applying `őď`
of the right unitor `tensorObjRightUnitor G` to the section multiplication `ő∑(a ‚äó 1)` returns `a`.

Reduction (blueprint): `ŌĀ_G = őĽ_G ‚ąė ő≤_{G,ūĚüô}`; split the application via `val_app_top_comp`, send
`ő∑(a ‚äó 1)` across the braiding to `ő∑(1 ‚äó a)` by `sectionMul_braiding_core`, then apply the
left-unitor core `unitor_sectionsMul`. -/
lemma sectionMul_rightUnitor_core (G : X.Modules) (a : ‚Ü•(G.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((tensorObjRightUnitor G).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((sectionsMul G (unitModule X)).hom
          (a ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))]
            (1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))))) = a := by
  rw [tensorObjRightUnitor_hom]
  rw [show ((tensorBraiding G (unitModule X)).hom ‚Čę (tensorObjUnitIso G).hom).val.app
        (Opposite.op ‚ä§)
      = ((tensorBraiding G (unitModule X)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)) ‚Čę
          ((tensorObjUnitIso G).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)) from rfl]
  show ((tensorObjUnitIso G).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
      (((tensorBraiding G (unitModule X)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((sectionsMul G (unitModule X)).hom
          (a ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))]
            (1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))))) = a
  rw [sectionMul_braiding_core G (unitModule X) a (1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))]
  exact unitor_sectionsMul G a

/-- **Unit-naturality at the top open** (project-local helper).  Applying `őď` (the top-open value)
of the sheafification `sheafification.map f` of a presheaf-of-modules morphism `f : P ‚ü∂ Q` to the
sheafification-unit image `ő∑_P(x)` returns the unit image `ő∑_Q(f(x))` of the transported element.
This is the single naturality square of the sheafification unit `ő∑`, evaluated at `‚ä§`; it is the
common engine of the section-ő∑ cores and is applied once per segment in `sectionMul_assoc_core`. -/
private lemma sheafification_map_unit_top {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ‚ü∂ Q)
    (x : ‚Ü•(P.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((sheafification.map f).val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P).app
          (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x)
      = ((((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app Q).app
          (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom ((f.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x)) := by
  have H := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.naturality f
  exact (congrArg (fun m => (m.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x) H).symm

/-- Inverse companion of `sheafification_map_unit_top`: when `sheafification.map f` is an
isomorphism, `őď(inv (sheafification.map f))` sends the unit image `ő∑_Q(f x)` back to the unit image
`ő∑_P(x)`.  Used for the inverse whiskered-unit segment (segment 1) of `tensorObjAssoc`. -/
private lemma sheafification_map_unit_top_inv {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ‚ü∂ Q)
    [IsIso (sheafification.map f)] (x : ‚Ü•(P.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((inv (sheafification.map f)).val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app Q).app
          (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom ((f.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x))
      = ((((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P).app
          (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom x) := by
  rw [‚Üź sheafification_map_unit_top f x, ‚Üź val_app_top_comp, IsIso.hom_inv_id]
  rfl

/-- **Associativity-ő∑ core** of associativity (`lem:sectionMul_assoc_core`).  Applying `őď` of the
sheaf associator `tensorObjAssoc A B C` to the left-bracketed iterated section multiplication
`ő∑((ŌÉ¬∑ŌĄ) ‚äó ŌÖ)` yields the right-bracketed one `ő∑(ŌÉ ‚äó (ŌĄ¬∑ŌÖ))`.

Mathematically (blueprint): both sides are iterated `ő∑`-images of elementary tensors; the sheaf
associator is the presheaf associator `őĪ_p` sheafified and conjugated by the whiskered-unit
comparison isos (`tensorObjAssoc`, `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`).  On an `ő∑`-image
those comparison isos act as the identity reparametrisation, so only `(őĪ_p)^#` contributes, and
`ő∑`-naturality against `őĪ_p` (which reassociates `(ŌÉ‚äóŌĄ)‚äóŌÖ ‚Ü¶ ŌÉ‚äó(ŌĄ‚äóŌÖ)` at the top open) closes it.

The reparametrisation-by-comparison-iso step (the conjugation in `tensorObjAssoc` segments 1/3
acting trivially on a unit image) is the remaining obstacle; it is the sheaf-level shadow of the
Mac Lane coherence transfer also needed by `tensorPowAdd_assoc`. -/
lemma sectionMul_assoc_core (A B C : X.Modules)
    (ŌÉ : ‚Ü•(A.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) (ŌĄ : ‚Ü•(B.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
    (ŌÖ : ‚Ü•(C.val.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) :
    ((tensorObjAssoc A B C).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        ((sectionsMul (tensorObj A B) C).hom
          (((sectionsMul A B).hom (ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ))
            ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÖ))
      = (sectionsMul A (tensorObj B C)).hom
          (ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))]
            ((sectionsMul B C).hom (ŌĄ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÖ))) := by
  -- The whiskered-unit segments (1 and 4) are isos by the strong-monoidality comparison.
  haveI i1 := isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B))
    ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C)
  haveI i2 := isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C))
    ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A)
  -- Expand `tensorObjAssoc` into its five segments and peel the top-open application of the
  -- composite into the five nested `őď(sheafification.map _)` (segment 1 inverted).
  simp only [tensorObjAssoc, Iso.trans_hom, val_app_top_comp, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom, asIso_inv,
    Functor.mapIso_hom]
  -- Segment 1 (inverse whiskered unit): sends the `sectionsMul` nest back to the triple-presheaf
  -- unit image `ő∑_{(a‚äó‚āöb)‚äó‚āöc}((ŌÉ‚äóŌĄ)‚äóŌÖ)`.
  have h1 : ((inv (sheafification.map (MonoidalCategory.whiskerRight (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
            (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
              ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B)))
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C)))).val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          ((sectionsMul (tensorObj A B) C).hom
            (((sectionsMul A B).hom (ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ))
              ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÖ))
        = (((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
            (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
              (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
                ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B))
              ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C))).app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
            ((ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ)
              ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÖ) :=
    sheafification_map_unit_top_inv (MonoidalCategory.whiskerRight (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
          (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B)))
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C))
      ((ŌÉ ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌĄ)
        ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] ŌÖ)
  erw [h1]
  -- Segments 2‚Äď5: unit naturality (associator, braiding, whiskered unit, braiding).
  erw [sheafification_map_unit_top (MonoidalCategory.associator (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C)).hom,
    sheafification_map_unit_top (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A)
        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
          ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C))).hom,
    sheafification_map_unit_top (MonoidalCategory.whiskerRight (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
          (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
            ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj B) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj C)))
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A)),
    sheafification_map_unit_top (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (tensorObj B C)) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj A)).hom]
  -- The remaining presheaf-level structural maps (associator, braiding, whiskered unit, braiding)
  -- evaluated at the top open compute definitionally on the elementary tensor `(ŌÉ‚äóŌĄ)‚äóŌÖ`, sending it
  -- to `ŌÉ ‚äó ő∑(ŌĄ‚äóŌÖ)`, which is the right-bracketed section multiplication.
  rfl

/-! ### Tensor-power comparison coherences (`lem:tensorPowAdd_{rightUnit,braiding,assoc}`)

These factor the comparison `őľ_{m,m'}` (`tensorPowAdd`) along the unit/braiding/associator
branches, transferring the symmetric-monoidal Mac Lane coherences of the presheaf monoidal
structure through the `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` comparison isos.  They are the
structural half of `lem:sectionMul_coherent`; the section-level cores above discharge the
sheafification-unit bookkeeping. -/

/-- In any braided monoidal category the braiding of the unit object with itself is the identity,
`ő≤_{ūĚüô,ūĚüô} = ūĚüô`.  (`braiding_tensorUnit_left` at `ūĚüô` plus `unitors_equal`.)  Project-local helper for
the base cases of the tensor-power comparison coherences. -/
private lemma braiding_unit_unit {C : Type*} [Category C] [MonoidalCategory C] [BraidedCategory C] :
    ő≤_ (ūĚüô_ C) (ūĚüô_ C) = Iso.refl _ := by
  apply Iso.ext
  rw [Iso.refl_hom, braiding_tensorUnit_left, MonoidalCategory.unitors_equal, Iso.hom_inv_id]

/-- The hand-built sheaf braiding of the unit module with itself is the identity.  Pure
sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf-level fact `braiding_unit_unit` (no monoidal structure
on `X.Modules` needed): the underlying presheaf of `unitModule X` is the presheaf monoidal unit. -/
private lemma tensorBraiding_unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) :
    tensorBraiding (unitModule X) (unitModule X) = Iso.refl _ := by
  have hb : BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X)) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X))
      = Iso.refl _ := braiding_unit_unit
  show sheafification.mapIso (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X))
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X))) = Iso.refl _
  rw [hb]
  exact Functor.mapIso_refl _ _

/-- The hand-built sheaf braiding is involutive: `ő≤_{F,G} ‚Čę ő≤_{G,F} = ūĚüô`.  Pure
sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf symmetry `SymmetricCategory.symmetry` (no monoidal
structure on `X.Modules` needed).  Used in the base case of `tensorPowAdd_braiding`. -/
private lemma tensorBraiding_symm (F G : X.Modules) :
    tensorBraiding F G ‚Č™‚Čę tensorBraiding G F = Iso.refl _ := by
  have hs : (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ‚Č™‚Čę
      (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F)) = Iso.refl _ := by
    apply Iso.ext; simp [SymmetricCategory.symmetry]
  show sheafification.mapIso (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ‚Č™‚Čę
      sheafification.mapIso (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F)) = Iso.refl _
  rw [‚Üź Functor.mapIso_trans]
  exact (congrArg sheafification.mapIso hs).trans (Functor.mapIso_refl _ _)

/-- **Right-unit branch** of the tensor-power comparison (`lem:tensorPowAdd_rightUnit`): `őľ_{n,0}`
factors as the right unitor `ŌĀ_{L^{‚äón}}` (`tensorObjRightUnitor`) followed by the index-equality
isomorphism `n = n + 0`.  Proved by induction on `n`; the successor step is the symmetric-monoidal
identity `ŌĀ_{A‚äóL} = (ŌĀ_A ‚Ė∑ L) ‚ąė őĪ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąė (A ‚óĀ ő≤) ‚ąė őĪ` transferred from the presheaf level. -/
lemma tensorPowAdd_rightUnit (L : X.Modules) (n : ‚Ąē) :
    tensorPowAdd L n 0 = tensorObjRightUnitor (tensorPow L n) ‚Č™‚Čę
      eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (add_zero n).symm) := by
  induction n with
  | zero =>
    -- Base: `őľ_{0,0}` is `őĽ_ūĚüô` (by `tensorPowAdd_zero`) and `ŌĀ_ūĚüô = őĽ_ūĚüô ‚ąė ő≤_{ūĚüô,ūĚüô}`.  After
    -- unfolding both unitors and matching the index isos, `congr 1` reduces the base case to the
    -- single residual `őĽ_ūĚüô = ő≤_{ūĚüô,ūĚüô} ‚Čę őĽ_ūĚüô`, i.e. `tensorBraiding ūĚüô ūĚüô = ūĚüô` (the symmetry on the
    -- unit object), the one remaining presheaf-level coherence fact for the base.
    rw [tensorPowAdd_zero, tensorObjRightUnitor]
    congr 1
    show tensorObjUnitIso (unitModule X)
        = tensorBraiding (unitModule X) (unitModule X) ‚Č™‚Čę tensorObjUnitIso (unitModule X)
    rw [tensorBraiding_unitModule, Iso.refl_trans]
  | succ k _ih =>
    -- Successor: expand `őľ_{k+1,0}` by the `tensorPowAdd` successor branch and use the IH; the
    -- residual is the Mac Lane right-unit coherence transferred through `tensorObjAssoc`.
    sorry

/-- **Commutativity branch** of the tensor-power comparison (`lem:tensorPowAdd_braiding`):
`őľ_{na,nb}` agrees with `őľ_{nb,na}` after braiding `ő≤_{L^{‚äóna},L^{‚äónb}}` and the index-equality
isomorphism `nb + na = na + nb`.  Proved by induction on `na`; transfers the hexagon coherence of
the presheaf symmetric monoidal structure. -/
lemma tensorPowAdd_braiding (L : X.Modules) (na nb : ‚Ąē) :
    tensorPowAdd L na nb = tensorBraiding (tensorPow L na) (tensorPow L nb) ‚Č™‚Čę
      tensorPowAdd L nb na ‚Č™‚Čę eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (add_comm nb na)) := by
  induction na with
  | zero =>
    -- Base: `őľ_{0,nb} = őĽ`, and `őľ_{nb,0} = ŌĀ = őĽ ‚ąė ő≤` (`tensorPowAdd_rightUnit`); the two
    -- braidings `ő≤_{ūĚüô,L^{‚äónb}}` / `ő≤_{L^{‚äónb},ūĚüô}` cancel by `tensorBraiding_symm`, leaving the
    -- left unitor with matching `eqToIso` reindexers (proof-irrelevant Nat equalities).
    show tensorPowAdd L 0 nb = tensorBraiding (unitModule X) (tensorPow L nb) ‚Č™‚Čę
      tensorPowAdd L nb 0 ‚Č™‚Čę eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (add_comm nb 0))
    rw [tensorPowAdd_zero, tensorPowAdd_rightUnit]
    simp only [tensorObjRightUnitor, Iso.trans_assoc]
    rw [‚Üź Iso.trans_assoc, tensorBraiding_symm (unitModule X) (tensorPow L nb), Iso.refl_trans]
    congr 1
  | succ k _ih =>
    -- Successor: hexagon coherence transferred through `tensorObjAssoc` + IH at index `k`.
    sorry

/-- **Associativity branch** of the tensor-power comparison (`lem:tensorPowAdd_assoc`): the two
bracketings of `L^{‚äóna} ‚äó L^{‚äónb} ‚äó L^{‚äónc}` agree after the index-equality isomorphism
`(na+nb)+nc = na+(nb+nc)`.  Proved by induction on `na`; transfers the pentagon coherence. -/
lemma tensorPowAdd_assoc (L : X.Modules) (na nb nc : ‚Ąē) :
    (tensorObjWhiskerRightIso (tensorPowAdd L na nb) (tensorPow L nc)).symm ‚Č™‚Čę
        tensorObjAssoc (tensorPow L na) (tensorPow L nb) (tensorPow L nc) ‚Č™‚Čę
        tensorObjWhiskerLeftIso (tensorPow L na) (tensorPowAdd L nb nc) ‚Č™‚Čę
        tensorPowAdd L na (nb + nc) =
      tensorPowAdd L (na + nb) nc ‚Č™‚Čę
        eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (add_assoc na nb nc)) := by
  induction na with
  | zero =>
    -- Base: reduces to the triangle identity relating left unitor and associator.
    sorry
  | succ k _ih =>
    -- Successor: pentagon coherence transferred through `tensorObjAssoc` + IH at index `k`.
    sorry

/-- Left unitality of the graded section multiplication (`lem:sectionMul_coherent`, left-unit case):
for `a ‚ąą őď(X, L^{‚äón})`, transporting `1 ¬∑ a` along `0 + n = n` gives `a`.
Mirrors `TensorPower.one_mul`.

Reduction: `őľ_{0,n}` is (by the `m = 0` branch of `tensorPowAdd`, `tensorPowAdd_zero`) the left
unitor `tensorObjUnitIso (L^{‚äón})` post-composed with the index reindex
`eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (Nat.zero_add n).symm)`.  Splitting `őď(őľ_{0,n})` along this
composite (`Iso.trans_hom` + `val_app_top_comp`) and cancelling the reindex against the outer
`sectionsCast` (`sectionsCast_eqToIso_cancel`) reduces the goal to the unitor-ő∑ core
`unitor_sectionsMul`. -/
theorem sectionsMul_one_mul (L : X.Modules) {n : ‚Ąē} (a : sectionDeg L n) :
    sectionsCast L (zero_add n) (GradedMonoid.GMul.mul GradedMonoid.GOne.one a) = a := by
  rw [gMul_def, tensorPowAdd_zero, Iso.trans_hom]
  -- Split off the index reindex `eqToIso` and align the element to the unit form, all by defeq
  -- (`show`, default transparency): `őď(unitor ‚Čę eqToIso)(ő∑(1¬∑a)) = őď(eqToIso)(őď(unitor)(ő∑(1¬∑a)))`.
  show sectionsCast L (zero_add n)
      (((eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (zero_add n).symm)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        (((tensorObjUnitIso (tensorPow L n)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          ((sectionsMul (unitModule X) (tensorPow L n)).hom
            ((1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)))
              ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] a)))) = a
  rw [sectionsCast_eqToIso_cancel]
  exact unitor_sectionsMul (tensorPow L n) a

/-- Right unitality of the graded section multiplication
(`lem:sectionMul_coherent`, right-unit case):
for `a ‚ąą őď(X, L^{‚äón})`, transporting `a ¬∑ 1` along `n + 0 = n` gives `a`.
Mirrors `TensorPower.mul_one`. -/
theorem sectionsMul_mul_one (L : X.Modules) {n : ‚Ąē} (a : sectionDeg L n) :
    sectionsCast L (add_zero n) (GradedMonoid.GMul.mul a GradedMonoid.GOne.one) = a := by
  rw [gMul_def, tensorPowAdd_rightUnit, Iso.trans_hom]
  -- Split off the index reindex `eqToIso` and align the element to the right-unit form (defeq).
  show sectionsCast L (add_zero n)
      (((eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (add_zero n).symm)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        (((tensorObjRightUnitor (tensorPow L n)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          ((sectionsMul (tensorPow L n) (unitModule X)).hom
            (a ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))]
              (1 : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))))))) = a
  rw [sectionsCast_eqToIso_cancel]
  exact sectionMul_rightUnitor_core (tensorPow L n) a

/-- Associativity of the graded section multiplication (`lem:sectionMul_coherent`, associativity):
transporting `(a ¬∑ b) ¬∑ c` along `(na + nb) + nc = na + (nb + nc)` gives `a ¬∑ (b ¬∑ c)`.
Mirrors `TensorPower.mul_assoc`. -/
theorem sectionsMul_mul_assoc (L : X.Modules) {na nb nc : ‚Ąē}
    (a : sectionDeg L na) (b : sectionDeg L nb) (c : sectionDeg L nc) :
    sectionsCast L (add_assoc na nb nc)
      (GradedMonoid.GMul.mul (GradedMonoid.GMul.mul a b) c) =
      GradedMonoid.GMul.mul a (GradedMonoid.GMul.mul b c) := by
  -- Unfold the degreewise multiplication on both bracketings into `őď(őľ) ‚ąė sectionsMul` nests.
  simp only [gMul_def]
  -- Remaining bridge (blueprint `lem:sectionMul_coherent`, associativity case): factor
  -- `őľ_{na+nb,nc}` and `őľ_{na,nb+nc}` via `tensorPowAdd_assoc`, cancel the `eqToIso` reindex
  -- against the outer `sectionsCast` (`sectionsCast_eq_eqToIso`/`sectionsCast_eqToIso_cancel`),
  -- and intertwine the two iterated section multiplications through `őď(őĪ)` by
  -- `sectionMul_assoc_core` ‚ÄĒ using the naturality of `sectionsMul` in each variable under the
  -- whiskerings `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso`/`tensorObjWhiskerLeftIso` that appear in
  -- `tensorPowAdd_assoc`.  The `sectionsMul`-whiskering naturality bricks are not yet in file.
  sorry

/-- Commutativity of the graded section multiplication (`lem:sectionMul_coherent`, commutativity):
transporting `a ¬∑ b` along `na + nb = nb + na` gives `b ¬∑ a`.
Section-level analogue of the `mul_comm` in `TensorPower.Basic`. -/
theorem sectionsMul_mul_comm (L : X.Modules) {na nb : ‚Ąē}
    (a : sectionDeg L na) (b : sectionDeg L nb) :
    sectionsCast L (add_comm na nb) (GradedMonoid.GMul.mul a b) =
    GradedMonoid.GMul.mul b a := by
  rw [gMul_def, tensorPowAdd_braiding, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom]
  -- Split `őď(ő≤ ‚Čę őľ_{nb,na} ‚Čę eqToIso)(ő∑(a‚äób))` into the three applications (defeq).
  show sectionsCast L (add_comm na nb)
      (((eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (add_comm nb na))).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
        (((tensorPowAdd L nb na).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
          (((tensorBraiding (tensorPow L na) (tensorPow L nb)).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom
            ((sectionsMul (tensorPow L na) (tensorPow L nb)).hom
              (a ‚äó‚āú[‚Ü•(X.sheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))] b))))) = GradedMonoid.GMul.mul b a
  -- Send `őď(ő≤)(ő∑(a‚äób))` to `ő∑(b‚äóa)` (braiding core), cancel the reindex, recognise `b ¬∑ a`.
  rw [sectionMul_braiding_core (tensorPow L na) (tensorPow L nb) a b,
    sectionsCast_eqToIso_cancel]
  rfl

/-! ### Graded-ring assembly (`lem:sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring`)

The section components `m ‚Ü¶ őď(X, L^{‚äóm})` carry a `DirectSum.GCommSemiring`, assembled
field-for-field as `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorPower.Basic` builds its graded semiring:
the graded-monoid layer from the four cast-mediated coherences via `gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast`,
and the bilinearity (`GSemiring`) fields for free from the `őď(X,ūĚí™_X)`-linearity of the
degreewise multiplication `őď(őľ) ‚ąė sectionsMul` (recorded by `gMul_def`). -/

/-- Graded-monoid structure on the section components: the unit and associativity coherences
are the cast-mediated identities `sectionsMul_{one_mul,mul_one,mul_assoc}` repackaged as
dependent-pair equalities by `gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast`.  `gnpow` is defaulted (as in
`TensorPower.Basic`). -/
noncomputable instance instGMonoid (L : X.Modules) : GradedMonoid.GMonoid (sectionDeg L) :=
  { (inferInstance : GradedMonoid.GMul (sectionDeg L)),
    (inferInstance : GradedMonoid.GOne (sectionDeg L)) with
    one_mul := fun _ => gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast L (zero_add _) (sectionsMul_one_mul L _)
    mul_one := fun _ => gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast L (add_zero _) (sectionsMul_mul_one L _)
    mul_assoc := fun _ _ _ =>
      gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast L (add_assoc _ _ _) (sectionsMul_mul_assoc L _ _ _) }

/-- Graded-semiring structure on the section components.  The bilinearity fields are free:
the degreewise multiplication `őď(őľ) ‚ąė sectionsMul` is `őď(X,ūĚí™_X)`-linear (it is a composite of
the bilinear section multiplication with the linear comparison map), so it annihilates `0` and
distributes over sums in each variable; `natCast n` is the `n`-fold sum of the degree-`0`
unit. -/
noncomputable instance instGSemiring (L : X.Modules) : DirectSum.GSemiring (sectionDeg L) :=
  { instGMonoid L with
    mul_zero := fun a => by
      rw [gMul_def]
      conv_rhs => rw [‚Üź map_zero ((tensorPowAdd L _ _).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom,
        ‚Üź map_zero (sectionsMul (tensorPow L _) (tensorPow L _)).hom]
      congr 2
      exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ a
    zero_mul := fun b => by
      rw [gMul_def]
      conv_rhs => rw [‚Üź map_zero ((tensorPowAdd L _ _).hom.val.app (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom,
        ‚Üź map_zero (sectionsMul (tensorPow L _) (tensorPow L _)).hom]
      congr 2
      exact TensorProduct.zero_tmul _ b
    mul_add := fun a b c => by
      rw [gMul_def, gMul_def, gMul_def, ‚Üź map_add, ‚Üź map_add]
      congr 2
      exact TensorProduct.tmul_add a b c
    add_mul := fun a b c => by
      rw [gMul_def, gMul_def, gMul_def, ‚Üź map_add, ‚Üź map_add]
      congr 2
      exact TensorProduct.add_tmul a b c
    natCast := fun n =>
      (n : ‚Ü•(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))) ‚ÄĘ (GradedMonoid.GOne.one : sectionDeg L 0)
    natCast_zero := by rw [Nat.cast_zero, zero_smul]
    natCast_succ := fun n => by rw [Nat.cast_succ, add_smul, one_smul] }

/-- Graded *commutative* semiring structure on the section components: graded commutativity is
the cast-mediated identity `sectionsMul_mul_comm` repackaged by `gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast`. -/
noncomputable instance instGCommSemiring (L : X.Modules) :
    DirectSum.GCommSemiring (sectionDeg L) :=
  { instGSemiring L with
    mul_comm := fun _ _ => gradedMonoid_eq_of_cast L (add_comm _ _) (sectionsMul_mul_comm L _ _) }

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **The section graded ring** (`lem:sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring`): the family of
`őď(X,ūĚí™_X)`-modules `m ‚Ü¶ őď(X, L^{‚äóm})` carries a `DirectSum.GCommSemiring`, hence
`‚®Ā m, őď(X, L^{‚äóm})` is a commutative semiring (the section graded ring `R(X,L)`).  The
degreewise multiplication is the section multiplication followed by the tensor-power comparison
